provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Fire and Rescue (Service) Regulations, 2007.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires “ Act ” means the Fire and Rescue Force Act; “ appointing authority ” means— (a) in relation to the power of appointment of fire and rescue officers above the rank of Assistant Commissioner, the President of the United Republic of Tanzania; (b) in relation to the power of appointment of fire and rescue officers of the rank of Assistant Inspector up to and including that of the Assistant Commissioner. (c) in relation to the power of appointment of fire and rescue officers below the rank of Assistant Inspector, the Commissioner-General ; or (d) any person or authority to whom power to make appointments and promotions has been delegated; " appointment " includes— (a) a temporary appointment; (b) an acting appointment where the person appointed to such acting appointment is, under the terms of his appointment, entitled to the salary of the post to which he is so appointed; (c) an acting appointment for more than fourteen days where the person is entitled to an acting allowance; " child " means— (a) an unmarried child, a child born out of wedlock, a step child, a legally adopted child or child of a deceased near relative who is not more than eighteen years of age and is wholly maintained by the fire and rescue officer ; (b) an unmarried child, a child born out of wedlock, a step child or a legally adopted child or child of deceased near relative of any age, who because of a physical or mental infirmity is unable to earn a living and is therefore wholly maintained by the fire and rescue officer ; (c) an unmarried child of any age who for reasons of undergoing studies at any recognized institution is unable to earn a living and is wholly maintained by the fire and rescue officer ; “ Commandant ” means a fire and rescue officer appointed by the Commissioner-General to be the head of any training institution in the Force ; “ Commissioner-General ” means a Commissioner-General appointed under section 14 of the Fire and Rescue Act ; “ delegate ” includes the Commissioners, Deputy Commissioners, Senior Assistant Commissioners, Regional Fire and Rescue Officers, District Fire and Rescue Officers and any other Fire and Rescue Officer to whom the powers have been delegated; “ disciplinary authority ” means an authority specified under section 7 of the Act ; “ disciplinary offence ” means any act, omission, misconduct, failure to take reasonable care or failure to discharge duties in a reasonable satisfactory manner by or on the part of a fire and rescue officer , which in the opinion of the disciplinary authority , warrants disciplinary proceedings under these Regulations; “ enlistment ” means to cause any person to become a member of the Fire and Rescue Force and includes re-engagement; “ family ” means a fire and rescue officer , his or her spouse and children of such an officer; if any; “ fire and rescue officer ” means a fireman appointed under the provisions of the Fire and Rescue Act ; “ gazetted officer ” means any fire and rescue officer who has attended and passed the gazetted officers course and has subsequently been promoted to the rank of Assistant Superintendent or above; “ gazetted officers course ” means an in-service fire and rescue officers' course leading to a promotion to a senior fire and rescue officer ; “ inquiry officer ” means the Commissioner-General or an officer appointed by the Commissioner-General to conduct an inquiry under these Regulations; “ inspector ” means an Inspector and includes an Assistant Inspector; “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for matters relating to the fire and rescue services; “ officer-in-charge ” means a fire and rescue officer appointed by the Commissioner-General to be in-charge of a fire station; “ Permanent Secretary ” means the Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for matters relating to the fire and rescue services; “ professional qualification ” means any recognized university degree or other recognized equivalent qualification from a recognized university or institution; “ Regional Fire and Rescue Officer ” means a Fire and Rescue Officer appointed by the Commissioner General to be the head of the fire and rescue services in the region; “ secondment ” means the attachment of a fire and rescue officer to any other public or private institution or post for specified period of time as may be determined by the appointing authority ; “ senior fire and rescue officer ” means a gazetted fire and rescue officer of or above the rank of Assistant Superintendent; “ Force ” means the Fire and Rescue Force established under section 4 of the Fire and Rescue Force Act ; “ junior fire and rescue officer ” means a Fire and rescue officer of or below the rank of Inspector; “ trade and non-professional qualification ” means any qualification or craft acquired by a fire and rescue officer from a recognized institution leading to the award of a certificate or diploma. Part II – Establishment and abolition of fire and rescue offices

provisions.title: Power to establish and abolish fire and rescue offices
provisions.content: (1) The power to establish and abolish offices in the Force is vested in the President. (2) A proposal for the establishment of an office in the Force shall be made to the President by the Permanent Secretary on the recommendation of the Commissioner-General . (3) A proposal for the abolition of an office in the Force shall be made to the President by the Permanent Secretary upon recommendation of the Commission. (4) Without prejudice to sub regulations (2) and (3) the President may establish or abolish any office in the Force as he deems fit. Part III – Appointments, enlistments, promotion, re-engagement and discharge

provisions.title: Application of the Act
provisions.content: The provisions of Part I of the Act relating to appointments, promotion, confirmation and termination of appointments, otherwise than by disciplinary proceedings, shall apply to all fire and rescue officers depending on the appointing authority .

provisions.title: Powers of the Commissioner-General
provisions.content: (1) The powers in respect of fire and rescue officers below the rank of Assistant Inspector in relation to appointment , promotion and termination of appointment , re-engagement for further service on or after completion of an initial period of service, retirement on or after reaching voluntary retirement age, shall be vested in the Commissioner-General . (2) The appointments, re-engagements, termination of appointments and remunerations of special staff shall be made by the Commissioner General as he shall deem proper.

provisions.title: Declaration on enlistment
provisions.content: (1) Every fire and rescue officer shall, on appointment to or enlistment in the Force , make a declaration of allegiance before a Senior Officer or a Commissioner for Oaths in such manner as he may declare to be most binding on his conscience, in such terms as maybe determined by the Commissioner General. (2) A person making allegiance under sub regulation (1) shall, before making the declaration, respond truthfully to any questions which may be put to him as to his previous employment or occupation, and as to whether he has at any time been convicted of a criminal offence. (3) Any person who willfully makes a false statement under sub regulation (2) commits an offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding fifty thousand shillings or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both.

provisions.title: Enlistment and re-engagement
provisions.content: (1) Enlistment in any rank below that of Assistant Inspector shall be for a period of three years or such less period as may be determined by the Commissioner-General . (2) Upon completion of the first period of service or within three months before such completion, the Commissioner-General or his delegate may approve re-engagement for a further period of three years or such less period as he may determine and may approve further re-engagement. (3) No person who is under the age of eighteen years shall be enlisted into the Force . (4) Where any non-pensionable fire and rescue officer below the rank of Assistant Inspector offers to re-engage for service within six months after having received his discharge shall, if there is a vacancy and the Commissioner-General approves, be entitled to the rank or grade held at the date of his discharge and his service be deemed to be continuous for the purposes of eligibility for gratuity provided for in the Act . (5) A non-pensionable fire and rescue officer below the rank of Assistant Inspector may be permitted to re-engage for service after the expiration of six months from the date of his discharge and in such a case the Commissioner-General may allow his previous service or part thereof to count for the purpose of eligibility for gratuity under the Act , and he may determine the rank or grade in which such officer shall be re-engaged.

provisions.title: Previous service may be taken into account for gratuity
provisions.content: (1) Where a non-pensionable Inspector, who has previously served in an approved service, the Immigration Service or the National Defence Forces of the United Republic is enlisted in the force within six months after having received his discharge from such previous service, he may be allowed to count for the purposes of the computation of the gratuity for which he may become eligible under the Act , his period of service in the Immigration Service and the National Defence Forces as if it had been in the Force . (2) In this regulation "approved service" means a fire and rescue service constituted similarly to the Force and approved by the Commissioner-General for the purposes of this regulation.

provisions.title: Admission to pensionable status
provisions.content: (1) A fire and rescue officer below the rank of Assistant Inspector who has completed not less than twelve years in continuous service may, on application to the Commissioner-General , be admitted to pensionable status. (2) A fire and rescue officer admitted to pensionable status under this regulation shall be confirmed in his appointment and be eligible to receive a gratuity under the Act .

provisions.title: Discharge on completion of service
provisions.content: (1) Subject to the provisions of these Regulations, every non-pensionable fire and rescue officer below the rank of Assistant Inspector shall, in accordance with the provisions of these Regulations, be discharged by the officer-in-charge at the place where the fire and rescue officer is stationed. (2) Notwithstanding sub regulation (1) , where at the date of such completion of service the fire and rescue officer is undergoing punishment for or stands charge with the commission of any offence against discipline under these Regulations, his service shall be prolonged and his discharge deferred until he has served the punishment or until he has been tried for the offence charged and has been acquitted or has served any punishment which may be imposed in respect of that offence or as may be determined by the appropriate authority.

provisions.title: Discharge on other grounds
provisions.content: (1) Any fire and rescue officer below the rank of Assistant Inspector may at any time, be discharged by the Commissioner General— (a) if, after investigation and after giving the fire and rescue officer an opportunity to make representations, the Commissioner General considers that he is unlikely to become or has ceased to be an efficient fire and rescue officer ; (b) if he is found by a government medical practitioner to be mentally or physically unfit for further service; (c) on dismissal from the force for misconduct; (d) on reduction of establishment; or (e) on purchase of his discharge, with the approval of the Commissioner General, at the rate of— (i) one month's gross salary during the first year of service; (ii) seventy five percent of monthly gross salary during the second year of service; (iii) fifty percent of monthly gross salary during the third or any subsequent year of service. (2) Notwithstanding sub regulation (1) the Commissioner-General shall have power to waive payment of the whole or part of the payment for purchase of discharge in such circumstances and upon such conditions as he may deem fit.

provisions.title: Calculation of service for the purpose of discharge
provisions.content: Upon calculating the service of a non-pensionable fire and rescue officer for the purposes of discharge there shall, in all cases, be excluded any period during which the fire and rescue officer has been absent from duty in any of the following circumstances— (a) detention awaiting-any trial which results in his acquittal or discharge; (b) desertion; and (c) absence without leave exceeding forty-eight hours.

provisions.title: Certificate of discharge
provisions.content: Every fire and rescue officer who is discharged under the preceding provisions shall be granted a certificate of discharge in such form as the Commissioner General may determine and shall, until he has received such certificate of discharge remain subject to the provisions of the Fire and Rescue Force Act . Part IV – Ranks and designation

provisions.title: Seniority of fire and rescue officers
provisions.content: (1) A senior fire and rescue officer shall take seniority over all junior fire and rescue officers. (2) Senior fire and rescue officers and junior fire and rescue officers shall among themselves take seniority in accordance with the specification of ranks and designation in the Force as provided in the Act . (3) A senior fire and rescue officer or a junior fire and rescue officer shall take seniority within his rank from the date of enlistment or promotion to the rank applicable, except where the Commissioner-General has granted seniority.

provisions.title: Order of seniority on reduction or promotion after reduction of rank
provisions.content: The Commissioner-General may prescribe the condition under which seniority may be adjusted on— (a) reduction of rank; and (b) promotion after reduction of rank.

provisions.title: Seniority from same date and command
provisions.content: (1) Where senior fire and rescue officers or junior fire and rescue officers hold the same rank with the same date of seniority, their seniority among themselves shall, unless the Commissioner-General otherwise directs, be determined by seniority among themselves shall, be determined by seniority in their immediate previous rank. (2) Unless the Commissioner-General otherwise directs, the order in which the names of fire and rescue officers appear in the current service register shall be the evidence of their relative seniorities. (3) Where it is not otherwise provided for under these Regulations, the senior fire and rescue officer present or in the absence of a senior fire and rescue officer , the senior junior fire and rescue officer present shall exercise command. Part V – Discipline

provisions.title: Discipline
provisions.content: Subject to the provisions of this Part, any offence against discipline or any other misconduct by a fire and rescue officer shall be dealt with in accordance with these Regulations.

provisions.title: Disciplinary authority in case of junior fire and rescue officers
provisions.content: (1) The powers of discipline in respect of junior fire and rescue officers of the rank below that of Assistant Inspector shall be exercised by the Commissioner-General or his delegate to the extent set out or otherwise in accordance with the provisions of this Part. (2) The powers of discipline in respect of junior fire and rescue officers of the rank of assistant inspector up to Inspector shall be exercised by the Commissioner-General and the final disciplinary authority is vested in the Commission.

provisions.title: Disciplinary authority in case of officer of, or above the rank of Assistant Superintendent
provisions.content: Subject to the provisions of section 7(3) of the Act , the disciplinary authority in the case of any fire and rescue officer of the rank of Assistant Superintendent up to and including that of Assistant Commissioner shall be the Commissioner-General , and the final disciplinary authority shall be vested in the Commission.

provisions.title: Disciplinary authority for officers of, or above the rank of Senior Assistant Commissioner
provisions.content: The disciplinary authority for officers of or above the rank of Senior Assistant Commissioner shall be the Permanent Secretary and the final disciplinary authority shall be the President as stipulated under section 7(2) of the Act .

provisions.title: Offences against discipline
provisions.content: Any fire and rescue officer who— (a) persuades or endeavours to persuade, procures or attempts to procure, or assist any fire and rescue officer to desert or being cognizant of any such desertion or intended desertion does not without delay give information thereof to his senior officer; (b) uses violence against a fire and rescue officer ; (c) is disrespectful in word, act or demeanour to his senior officer; (d) refuses to obey or willfully disobeys or neglects to obey any lawful command; (e) renders himself unfit for duty by excessive use of intoxicating liquor or stupefying drugs; (f) drinks intoxicating liquor on duty; (g) is drunk on duty or in any lines of the Force or in any place whilst wearing a uniform; (h) absent himself without leave; (i) all asleep whilst on duty; (j) being under arrest or in confinement, leaves or escapes from his arrest or confinement before he is set at liberty; (k) is guilty of cowardice; (l) fires any firearm without order or just cause; (m) without cause fails to appear at any parade appointed by his superior officer; (n) pawns, sells, loses by neglect, makes away with, willfully damages or fails to report any damage to any arm, ammunitions, accoutrement, uniform or other article supplied to him or any property committed to his charge; (o) is in unlawful possession of any public property or any property of any other member of the Force or any property of an individual; (p) solicits or accepts any fee, bribe or gratuity; (q) is idle or negligent in the performance of his duty; (r) is slovenly, inattentive, uncivil or quarrelsome; (s) parades for duty dirty or untidy in his person, arms, clothing or equipment; (t) without due authority discloses or conveys any information concerning any Force matter; (u) makes or signs any false statement in any official record or document; (v) makes or joins in making any anonymous complaint; (w) malingers or feigns or willfully causes any disease; (x) incurs any debt in or out of the Force which he is unable to discharge; (y) without proper authority extracts from any person carriage, porterage or provisions; (z) resists an escort whose duty it is to apprehend him or have him in charge; (aa) makes any false statement on becoming a fire and rescue officer ; (bb) refuses or neglects to make or send and report or return which it is his duty to make or send; (cc) makes a false accusation against any fire and rescue officer or member of the public knowing such accusation to be false; (dd) engages without authority in any employment other than his duty as a fire and rescue officer ; (ee) becomes security for any person or engages in any loan transaction with any fire and rescue officer without the permission in writing of the Commissioner-General ; (ff) commits, causes or permits any act to be committed or connives at any act which is prejudicial to the administration, discipline and efficiency of the Force or to the authority or position of a member of the Force ; (gg) makes use of his position in the Force to promote or to prejudice the interests of any political party; (hh) withholds, misapplies, unlawfully uses, loses by neglect or willfully damages state property of another member, mess, canteen, library, fund, club or other property belonging to a client of the Force ; or (ii) act or omission involving corrupt practices; (jj) does an act of insubordination; (kk) uses without consent of the prescribed authority any property or facilities belonged to the Force for some purposes not connected with the Force ; (ll) engages in any activity outside his official duties which is likely to lead to his taking improper advantage of his position in the Force ; (mm) refuses to comply with an order or is malingering regarding a posting to a station; (nn) fails to perform satisfactorily any duty imposed upon him; or (oo) is guilty of any act, conduct, disorder or neglect to the prejudice of good order or discipline of the Force or security of the Force premises.

provisions.title: Procedure at inquiries into disciplinary offences of fire and rescue officers below the rank of Assistant Inspector
provisions.content: (1) Where it appears to a senior fire and rescue officer that there is a prima facie case against a junior fire and rescue officer below the rank of Assistant Inspector, hereinafter referred to as "the defaulter", for a disciplinary offence , he shall frame a charge against the defaulter. (2) The senior fire and rescue officer aforesaid may— (a) proceed to hear the charge himself; or (b) report the charge to the Commissioner-General , who may proceed to hear the charge or remit the case to the reporting officer with instructions to hear the charge or appoint another senior fire and rescue officer to hear the charge. (3) The senior fire and rescue officer shall inform the defaulter of the day upon which the charge against him shall be brought up and the defaulter shall be required to appear and defend himself. (4) The charge shall first be read over to the defaulter who shall be required to plead guilty or not guilty to each charge separately. (5) The defaulter shall be given copies of or reasonable access to such records and other documents as he requires, which are necessary to enable him to prepare his defence, other than any records or documents in respect of which the Attorney-General is of the opinion that privilege could be claimed before a court. (6) The senior fire and rescue officer shall have the right to call such witnesses as he considers necessary for the proper hearing of the case and the defaulter shall have the right to call such witnesses as are reasonably required for his defence. (7) Where a witness is examined by a senior fire and rescue officer , the defaulter shall be given an opportunity to put questions on his own behalf to the witness, and no documentary evidence shall be used against him unless he has been previously supplied with a copy thereof or given access thereto. (8) Evidence shall not be taken on oath. (9) At the conclusion of the hearing, the senior fire and rescue officer shall announce his findings on each charge or may reserve his findings and as soon as the senior fire and rescue officer announces his findings he may make an award which he shall communicate personally to the defaulter. (10) The Commissioner-General may, at any time before a finding has been communicated to an accused junior fire and rescue officer , direct the senior fire and rescue officer to review his findings upon any grounds he thinks fit (11) Notwithstanding sub regulation (9) , the Commissioner-General shall not authorize the review of a finding of not guilty. (12) Upon any review under sub-regulation (9) , the senior fire and rescue officer may re-hear the case in whole or part and may take further evidence. (13) The defaulter may be allowed to bring any other officer to assist him in his defence. (14) No advocate may appear before any inquiry on behalf of the defaulter.

provisions.title: Punishment of junior fire and rescue officer of the rank below Assistant Inspector
provisions.content: (1) The punishment hereunder may be awarded by a senior fire and rescue officer on a junior fire rescue officer of the rank below Assistant Inspector found guilty of a disciplinary offence — (a) reprimand or severe reprimand; (b) extra duties or fatigues not exceeding four days; (c) a fine not exceeding one-quarter of one month's pay; (d) stoppage, withholding or deferment of any increment of salary for which the officer may be eligible; (e) reduction in rank or seniority; (f) in the case of a charge relating to damage or loss of Government property or a property of the Force , payment of the value of the property damaged or lost; (g) transfer on misconduct; (h) termination of appointment ; or (i) dismissal (2) Where a delegate awards a punishment under this regulation which amounts to dismissal, reduction of rank, transfer on misconduct, termination of appointment , payment of the value of the property damaged or lost and stoppages of salary increment, the punishment shall be subject to confirmation by the Commissioner-General . (3) A fire and rescue officer who is transferred as a result of misconduct shall not be paid transfer allowance other than transport assistance from old station to a new one. (4) Where any punishment awarded by a senior fire and rescue officer under the provisions of this regulation requires the confirmation of the Commissioner-General , the Commissioner-General may, vary or remit any punishment so awarded. (5) Notwithstanding sub regulation (4) , a punishment shall not be increased unless the accused has been given an opportunity to be heard by the Commissioner-General.

provisions.title: Procedure at inquiries into disciplinary offences of inspectors
provisions.content: (1) Where it appears to the senior fire and rescue officer under whose command an Inspector is that there is a prima facie case against the Inspector he shall inform the Inspector and require him to submit his representations in writing before him on a day to be specified. (2) After the expiration of the period specified under sub regulation (1) , the senior fire and rescue officer shall report the facts of the case together with any representation received from the Inspector to the Commissioner-General. (3) The Commissioner-General shall determine whether or not the charge is to be preferred against the Inspector. (4) Where the charge is preferred against the Inspector, the charge shall be served upon the Inspector and a notice be given to him that he is required to plead guilty or not guilty to such charge in writing within seven days. (5) Where the Inspector pleads guilty to a charge, he may communicate in writing with his plea any matter which he desires to be taken into consideration, and, if such plea is accepted by the Commissioner-General , such charge shall be dealt with by the Commissioner-General . (6) Notwithstanding sub regulation (5) , in any other case, the Commissioner-General shall either proceed to inquire into the charge himself or appoint one or more senior Fire and Rescue Officers to do so. (7) The Commissioner-General may appoint a prosecutor for any inquiry and the Inspector charged shall, whether a prosecutor is appointed or not, have the right to be represented by another Inspector or, with the approval of the Commissioner General, a senior Fire and Rescue Officer who may conduct his defence on his behalf. (8) Notwithstanding sub regulation (7) , an advocate may appear before any inquiry on behalf of an Inspector charged. (9) The Inspector charged shall be given copies of or reasonable access to such records and other documents as he requires, which are necessary to enable him to prepare his defence other than any records or documents in respect of which the Commissioner-General is of the opinion that privilege could be claimed before a court. (10) The Inquiry Officer shall inform the Inspector charged of the day upon which the charge against him shall be heard and he shall be required to appear before the Inquiry Officer to defend himself. (11) Where a witness is examined by the Inquiry officer, the Inspector charged shall be given an opportunity to put questions to the witness. (12) A documentary evidence shall not be used against the Inspector charged unless he has been previously supplied with a copy of, or given access to, the document. (13) Evidence shall not be taken on oath. (14) (a) Where the hearing of the inquiry has been concluded, the Inquiry officer shall, if he is-the Commissioner General, give his findings immediately and, if he finds the Inspector guilty of any of the charges against him he shall: (i) make an award or reserve his finding and award or his award only; or (ii) report the case to the Permanent Secretary together with a record of all the proceedings and his findings and recommendation for dismissal if he is satisfied that the circumstances of the case are such as to merit dismissal; and the Permanent Secretary may dismiss the Inspector or award any lesser punishment or remit the case to the Commissioner General, in which case, the Commissioner General may award any punishment which he has power to award under regulation 26 ; (b) a senior fire and rescue officer other than the Commissioner General, forward the record of inquiry together with his findings and recommendations to the Commissioner General who may— (i) confirm all or any of the findings or substitute for any finding at which the Inquiry officer could have arrived upon the evidence and make an award, or, if he considers that the circumstances of the case are such as to warrant dismissal, he shall proceed in accordance with the provisions of subparagraph (a) ; or (ii) quash any finding and acquit the Inspector charged or order a retrial on such charge or charges as he may specify. (15) Where an Inspector has pleaded guilty in writing and such plea is accepted by the Commissioner General, it shall operate in all respects as a finding of guilty by the Inquiry Officer.

provisions.title: Punishment of inspectors
provisions.content: (1) The punishments prescribed under regulation 24 may be awarded to an Inspector who pleads guilty or has been found guilty of an offence against discipline. (2) Notwithstanding any provision of these Regulations, where the Commissioner-General is of the opinion that the gravity of any charge which is found to have been proved against the Inspector warrants the infliction of any of the following punishments— (a) dismissal, that is termination of appointment with forfeiture of all retirement benefits in accordance with the provisions of any law for the time being in force; (b) termination of appointment otherwise than by dismissal; (c) reduction in rank, that is removal to another rank with an immediate reduction in salary; or (d) reduction in salary, he shall submit to the Permanent Secretary a report on the investigation of the charge together with details of any matter which his opinion aggravate or alleviate the gravity of the case. (3) Where the report is submitted by the Commissioner-General , the Permanent Secretary shall consider the report and may, if he is of the opinion that the report should be amplified or that further investigation is desirable, refer the matter back to the Commissioner-General for further investigation and report and after considering any further report, he shall determine the punishment, if any, to be inflicted and inform the accused officer of such determination. Disciplinary Proceedings: senior fire and rescue officers

provisions.title: Types of proceedings
provisions.content: Disciplinary proceedings in relation to senior fire and rescue officers may be either formal or summary.

provisions.title: Cases where formal proceedings applicable
provisions.content: Formal proceedings shall be instituted if, in the opinion of the disciplinary authority , the disciplinary offence which the fire and rescue officer is alleged to have committed is of such gravity as to warrant dismissal, termination of appointment , reduction in rank or salary.

provisions.title: Cases where summary proceedings applicable
provisions.content: Summary proceedings shall be instituted if, in the opinion of the disciplinary authority , the disciplinary offence which the officer is alleged to have committed is not of such gravity as to warrant dismissal, termination of appointment , reduction in rank or salary.

provisions.title: Commencement of formal proceedings
provisions.content: (1) No formal proceedings for a disciplinary offence shall be instituted against a senior fire and rescue officer , unless he has been served with a copy of the charge stating the nature of the offence which he is alleged to have committed (2) The charge shall be prepared by the disciplinary authority and he may: (a) consult the Attorney-General on the formulation of the charge; (b) order the conduct of preliminary investigation as he may consider necessary. (3) Every disciplinary charge shall state the nature of the offence which the accused is alleged to have committed, and shall specify the allegations made against the accused officer. (4) Where a disciplinary charge has been drawn up, the disciplinary authority shall cause the same to be served upon the officer. (5) Every charge shall be accompanied by a notice addressed to the accused officer inviting him to state in writing within such period as may be specified in the notice the ground upon which he relies to exculpate himself. (6) No formal proceedings instituted under these regulations shall be invalid by reason only of any irregularity in the formulation of the charge. (7) The powers conferred upon the disciplinary authority by this regulation may, if the Commissioner is a disciplinary authority , be exercised by the Permanent Secretary under whom the accused officer serves.

provisions.title: Inquiry into disciplinary charge
provisions.content: (1) Where an accused officer has been served with a charge pursuant to regulation 30 and fails to make representations in writing giving the ground upon which he relies to exculpate himself within the period specified in the notice accompanying the charge or make representations which, in the opinion of the disciplinary authority or the Permanent Secretary acting under the authority of regulation 30 (7) do not amount to a complete defence for the offence with which the accused officer is charged, the disciplinary authority or the Permanent Secretary as the case may be, shall conduct an inquiry into the charge. (2) Every inquiry into a charge under sub-regulation (1) shall be in accordance with the procedure prescribed under these Regulations. (3) Notwithstanding sub-regulation (2) , where the inquiry is conducted by the disciplinary authority or the Permanent Secretary, the disciplinary authority or the Permanent Secretary shall not be bound to follow the procedure prescribed under these Regulations but may hold the inquiry and conduct the investigation according to such procedure as he may consider appropriate. (4) Where the disciplinary authority or the Permanent Secretary is satisfied that the representations made by the accused officer pursuant to the notice served upon him under regulation 30 amount to a complete admission of the charge against him, it shall not be necessary to hold any further inquiry or the Permanent Secretary shall record a finding of guilty and the proceedings shall proceed as if the accused officer has been found guilty after an inquiry.

provisions.title: Certain persons may be appointed as inquiry officers
provisions.content: (1) Where the officer, other than the Commission, conducting the inquiry is of the opinion that it will be desirable for him to be assisted in the conduct of the inquiry by persons who may be more conversant with any professional or technical matter likely to arise in the course of the proceedings, he may request the Commission to assign no more than two public officers with the necessary qualifications or experience to assist him, and the Commission shall comply with any such request. (2) The officer conducting the inquiry shall notify the accused officer of the day, date, time and place upon which the inquiry shall be held. (3) The accused officer shall have a right to be present, examine witnesses and be heard at the inquiry; (4) Notwithstanding subregulation (3) , failure by the accused officer to be present or represented at the inquiry shall not invalidate the proceedings. (5) The officer conducting the inquiry may permit the accused officer and the disciplinary authority to be represented by any public officer or advocate. (6) The accused officer or where he is represented by a public officer or an advocate shall have a right to— (a) cross-examine any witness examined by the officer conducting the inquiry or by the disciplinary authority or his representative; (b) examine and make copies of any document produced as evidence against him; (c) call witnesses on his own behalf and produce any document relevant to the inquiry. (7) The officer conducting the inquiry may take into consideration any evidence which he considers relevant to the subject of the inquiry before him, notwithstanding that such evidence would not be admissible under the law relating to evidence. (8) The officer conducting the inquiry shall record the list of the evidence adduced before him. (9) Any inquiry heard under this regulation shall not be open to the public. (10) Where during the course of inquiry it appears that the evidence adduced discloses grounds for an additional charge, the disciplinary authority or his representative may cause such additional charge to be formulated and shall give a copy of the additional charge to the accused officer. (11) Where any additional charge is formulated in accordance with the provisions of sub-regulation (10) , the officer conducting the inquiry shall process to inquire into the same and may recall any witness for further examination or cross-examination. (12) Subject to the foregoing provisions of this regulation an officer conducting the inquiry may regulate the procedure at the inquiry in such manner as he may think fit. (13) In this regulation and regulation 34 , "officer conducting the inquiry" means the Inquiry Officer and includes, in cases where the inquiry is being conducted by the Commission, such Commission.

provisions.title: Procedure on the conclusion of inquiry
provisions.content: (1) Upon the conclusion of an inquiry, the officer conducting the inquiry shall, save where such officer himself is the disciplinary authority , forward the record of proceedings before him together with his report to the disciplinary authority . (2) The report under sub-regulation (1) shall state— (a) whether in the opinion of the officer conducting the inquiry the charge against the accused officer has been proved; (b) reasons for such opinion; (c) any fact which, in the opinion of the officer conducting the inquiry, aggravates or mitigates the gravity of the act or omission which was the subject; (d) any other fact which in the opinion of the officer conducting the inquiry is relevant. (3) Notwithstanding sub regulation (2) , the report shall not contain any recommendation as to the form or nature of the punishment to be awarded. (4) Where, on receipt of the record of proceedings and the report of the officer conducting the inquiry, the disciplinary authority is of the opinion that further investigation into the case is necessary, he may refer the matter back to the officer conducting the inquiry with such directions as he may consider necessary and such officer shall, upon receiving the record of proceedings, re-open the inquiry and proceed in accordance with regulation 31 . (5) Upon receipt of the record of proceedings and the report of, if the disciplinary authority himself conducted the inquiry, conclusion of the inquiry, the disciplinary authority shall, after considering the evidence and if the inquiry was not conducted by him, the report of the officer conducting the inquiry, make and record a finding whether, in his opinion, the accused officer is guilty of the disciplinary offence with which he was charged. (6) Where the findings of the disciplinary authority as to the guilty or innocence of the accused is contrary to the opinion of the officer conducting the inquiry as expressed in his report, the disciplinary authority shall record his reasons for the finding. (7) Where the disciplinary authority finds the accused officer guilty, he shall proceed to award punishment. (8) Notwithstanding sub regulation (6) , where the disciplinary authority is a person exercising powers conferred upon him by regulation 20 of these Regulations, he shall not impose any of the following punishments— (a) dismissal; (b) termination of appointment ; (c) reduction in rank; or (d) reduction in salary (9) Without prejudice to regulation (7) , where in his opinion the case is of such gravity as to warrant imposition of any of the punishments under regulation (7) , the disciplinary authority shall refer the case to the Permanent Secretary who shall proceed to award such punishment as he may consider appropriate.

provisions.title: Procedure in summary proceedings
provisions.content: (1) Where the disciplinary authority decides to institute summary proceedings against an accused officer, he shall cause a statement giving particulars of the charge to be prepared and served upon the accused officer. (2) The disciplinary authority shall appoint the date, time and place for the investigation of the charge and shall give the accused officer notice. (3) The accused officer shall have a right to be present at the investigation and make his defence. (4) Notwithstanding regulation (3) , failure by the accused officer to be present at the investigation shall not invalidate the proceedings. (5) The investigation of the charge shall be carried out in such manner as the disciplinary authority may determine. (6) Upon the conclusion of the investigation, the disciplinary authority shall make a finding whether in his opinion the accused officer is guilty of the offence with which he is charged, and if the disciplinary authority finds the accused officer guilty, he shall proceed to award such punishment as he may consider appropriate. (7) Notwithstanding regulation (6) , the disciplinary authority shall not, on investigation under this regulation, award to the accused officer the following punishments— (a) dismissal; (b) termination of appointment ; (c) reduction in rank; or (d) reduction in salary. (7) The disciplinary authority may authorize any officer who is on pensionable terms and holds a rank senior to the rank held by the accused officer to exercise all or any of his powers under sub-regulations (1) , (2) , (3) and (4) . [Please note: Numbering as in original.] (8) Where an officer authorized in accordance with regulation (7) holds an investigation— (a) the provisions of sub-regulations (1) , (2) , (3) and (4) shall apply as if references therein to the disciplinary authority were references to the officer so authorized; (b) he shall, upon the conclusion of the investigation, submit his report to the disciplinary authority who shall proceed to make a finding as to the guilty or innocence of the accused officer and in the event of the accused officer being found guilty, proceed to award punishment subject to the limitations imposed by the provisions of sub-regulation (6) . (9) Where an investigation under this regulations is being or has been held, the disciplinary authority may at any time before awarding punishment, institute formal proceedings against the accused officer if, in the opinion of the disciplinary authority , the evidence adduced discloses fact which adds the gravity of the charge or which show that the accused officer has or may have committed other disciplinary offence warranting formal proceedings.

provisions.title: Punishment of senior officers
provisions.content: (1) The following punishments may be imposed on a senior officer as a result of proceedings under this Part— (a) dismissal, that is, termination of appointment with forfeiture of all retirement benefits and other privileges in accordance with the provisions of any law for the time being in force; (b) termination of appointment otherwise than by dismissal; (c) reduction in rank, that is, removal to another rank with an immediate reduction in salary; (d) reduction in salary; (e) stoppage of increment, that is, non-payment for a specified period of an increment otherwise due; (f) fine of an amount not exceeding one quarter of a month's salary; (g) written reprimand; (h) written warning; (i) oral warning; (j) the recovery of the amount of any loss caused to the Government or the value of any property of the Government or the value of any property of the Government destroyed or damaged or the cost of repairing any property destroyed or damaged by the officer by an act or by reason of his failure to take reasonable care or to discharge his duties in a reasonable manner; (k) in the case of an officer who is the holder of a non-pensionable post, suspension from duty with consequent loss of pay and allowances for a period not exceeding fourteen days. (2) Where the punishment specified in paragraph (b) of sub-regulation (1) is to be imposed to a pensionable officer, the disciplinary authority shall invoke the procedure for retirement by age or retirement in public interest as per prevailing public policy. (3) Where an officer, by his act, causes the Government to suffer any loss or causes damage to any property of the Government, the amount of such loss or the value of the property damaged or an amount equal to the cost of repairing such property or such portion of such amount or value may, as the appropriate authority may determine, be recovered from such officer under the provision of the Public Officers (Recovery of Debts) Act . (4) Where steps have been taken to recover any of the amount or value as provided for under the Public Officers (Recovery of Debts) Act , it shall not be unlawful for disciplinary proceedings for the act to be instituted under these Regulations. (5) Without prejudice to sub regulation (4) , where the proceedings are instituted, the punishment specified in subparagraph (j) of sub-regulation (1) of this regulation shall not be awarded, save to the extent of the difference between the actual amount of the loss incurred by the Government or the actual value of the property or the actual cost of repairing such property, and the amount to be authorized to be recovered under the Public Officers (Recovery of Debts) Act . (6) Where an officer who has become liable to pay any amount to the Government by reasons specified under this regulation or is convicted under section 284A of the Penal Code for the act or omission or other circumstances giving rise to that liability, and the court has ordered the officer to pay to the Government compensation under subsection (7) of section 284A, any amount ordered to be recovered from the officer by a disciplinary authority under subparagraph (j) of sub-regulation (1) or by an appropriate authority under provisions of the Public Officers (Recovery of Debts) Act , the order of the disciplinary authority or, as the case may be, of the appropriate authority— (a) if the amount ordered to be recovered by the disciplinary authority or the appropriate authority, exceeds the amount of the compensation ordered under section 284A of the Penal Code , shall be effective only to the extent of the difference between the two amounts; or (b) if the amount ordered to be recovered by the disciplinary authority , is less than the amount of the compensation ordered under section 284A of the Penal Code , shall cease to have effect. (7) Notwithstanding the provisions of sub-regulation (3) , where disciplinary proceedings are instituted or an officer is punished under these Regulations after his conviction under section 284A of the Penal Code , it shall be lawful for the disciplinary authority to order that an amount equal to the difference between the actual amount of the loss, the value of the property of the cost or repairs and— (a) the amount of the compensation awarded under section 284A of the Penal Code ; or (b) where steps have also been taken to recover any amount from the officer under the Public Officers (Recovery of Debts) Act , the aggregate of the amount which may, subject to the provisions of sub-regulation (3) , be recovered under that Act and the amount of compensation awarded under section 284A of the Penal Code , be recovered from the officer by way of punishment. (8) In this regulation the term "appropriate authority" shall have the meaning assigned to it in the Public Officers (Recovery of Debts) Act .

provisions.title: Appeals
provisions.content: (1) Any fire and rescue officer of the rank of Assistant Inspector up to and including that of the Assistant Commissioner aggrieved by an award made by or confirmed by the Commissioner may appeal to the Commission. (2) Upon such appeal, the Commission may either allow or dismiss the appeal or may uphold the finding and confirm or remit the punishment or substitute any other punishment which could have been imposed in accordance with regulation 24 . (3) Any fire and rescue officer of the rank above Assistant Commissioner aggrieved by an award made by or confirmed by the Commission may, within seven days of the notification to him of the award, appeal in writing against the award to the President. (4) Any fire and rescue officer below the rank of Assistant Inspector, aggrieved by any finding or award made by the officer in-charge, the Regional Fire and Rescue Officer or any other senior officer may, within seven days of the notification to him of the award or finding, appeal in writing to the Commissioner-General and the Commissioner-General may confirm or vary any finding or remit any punishment awarded and in all such cases the decision of the Commissioner-General shall be final. (5) Where the Commissioner General hears any new evidence on appeal, he shall give the appellant an opportunity of being present and putting questions to any witnesses so heard. (6) In every case in which an appeal is lodged, the punishment shall be suspended during the hearing of the appeal.

provisions.title: Rules where disciplinary offence amounts to criminal offence
provisions.content: (1) Where an officer is alleged to have committed a disciplinary offence and the act or omission constituting such offence also constitutes a criminal offence under any written law, the following shall apply— (a) where no disciplinary proceedings under these Regulations have been commenced in respect of the disciplinary offence , no disciplinary proceedings shall be commenced until after the conclusion of the criminal proceedings; (b) where disciplinary proceedings have been commenced and during the pendency of such proceedings the criminal proceedings are instituted, the disciplinary proceedings shall be stayed and no further step shall be taken in respect of that disciplinary proceeding until after the conclusion of the criminal proceedings; (c) where criminal proceedings have been commenced and concluded against the accused officer and the accused officer— (i) is acquitted of the criminal charge on a legal technicality, the acquittal shall not be a bar to disciplinary proceedings against the accused officer on the same charge or on a charge based on the same facts being continued or instituted against the accused officer, and such disciplinary proceedings may be continued or instituted against the officer and he may be punished for that disciplinary offence as if no criminal proceedings had been instituted against him; (ii) is convicted of the criminal charge, the disciplinary authority may suspend the officer from the exercise of the powers and functions of his office pending consideration of his case under the provisions of this regulation; (d) where the officer has been convicted of a criminal offence, he shall be deemed to have been found guilty under the provisions of these Regulations of a disciplinary offence based upon the same charge or the same facts which formed the ground of the criminal charge of which he was convicted, and the disciplinary authority shall, without continuing or instituting any disciplinary proceedings, proceed to punish the officer of the disciplinary offence provided that— (i) no officer shall be punished under this paragraph until after he has been served with a notice specifying— (aa) the disciplinary offence of which he is deemed, under the provision of this paragraph, to have been found guilty; (bb) the punishment which the disciplinary authority proposes to impose; (cc) the time within which that officer may make such plea in mitigation as he may consider fit, and without the disciplinary authority having taken into consideration any plea made by the officer within the time specified in the notice; (ii) where the accused officer has been convicted of a criminal offence which may, in the opinion of the disciplinary authority , warrant his dismissal from the Force , disciplinary proceedings shall be instituted against the officer or where such proceedings were instituted prior to the commencement of the criminal proceedings, they shall be continued and the accused officer shall subject to the provisions of subparagraph (f) be tried in accordance with regulations 23 , 25 and 30 ; (e) where a notice under paragraph (i) of the proviso to subparagraph (d) is given to the officer, the disciplinary authority shall not be bound to impose the same punishment as specified in the notice but may impose any lesser punishment; (f) in any disciplinary proceedings under paragraph (ii) of the proviso to subparagraph (d) , proof that the accused officer has been convicted of the criminal offence shall be conclusive evidence of the fact that he has committed the disciplinary offence based on the same facts; (g) where an officer has been convicted of a criminal offence and prior to the commencement of the criminal proceedings he was found guilty by a disciplinary authority of a disciplinary offence based on the facts upon which the subsequent criminal charge was based, it shall be lawful for the disciplinary authority , upon the conclusion of the criminal proceedings, to substitute for the disciplinary offence any other disciplinary punishment which, in the opinion of the disciplinary authority is warranted in view of the conviction of the officer of the criminal offence or in view of any evidence adduced at the criminal proceedings. (2) For the purpose of this regulation, criminal proceedings shall be deemed to have been concluded— (a) where there has been no appeal against the conviction or acquittal, upon the expiry of the time allowed for such appeal by or under any written law; (b) where there has been an appeal or a further appeal against any conviction or acquittal, upon the expiry of time allowed by or under any written law for any further appeal. (3) Where there has been an appeal or a further appeal against any conviction or acquittal, the judgment of appellate court shall be taken into consideration in determining whether the officer has been convicted or acquitted. (4) Nothing in sub-regulation (1)(d)(i) of this regulation shall be construed as precluding the disciplinary authority from instituting disciplinary proceedings against the officer convicted of a criminal offence independent of his conviction of such criminal offence: Provided that where a disciplinary authority instituted disciplinary proceedings against an officer independent of this conviction of a criminal offence, then in the event of an appeal or further appeal against such conviction, the provisions of paragraphs (a), (b) and (c) of sub-regulation shall apply. (5) Where an accused officer is convicted of a criminal charge involving fraud or dishonesty on his part, an accused officer is, pursuant to sub-regulation (1)(d)(ii) , punished by dismissal from the Force for a disciplinary offence based on the same facts after formal proceedings having been taken against him, his dismissal shall take effect from the date upon which he was convicted of the criminal offence.

provisions.title: Reprimands and warnings
provisions.content: Nothing in these Regulations shall be construed as prohibiting or restricting disciplinary authority or his delegate from issuing a reprimand for a warning for unsatisfactory work or conduct to any fire and rescue officer directly junior to him.

provisions.title: Desertion
provisions.content: (1) Any enlisted fire and rescue officer who while still a member of the Force deserts from the Force shall be taken before a district court and tried for an offence against this regulation. (2) Where a fire and rescue officer is taken before a district court under this regulation, he shall be tried in the same manner as if he were charged with any other offence and shall be liable on conviction to a fine of not exceeding three months of his salary or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding six months or to both: Provided that no proceedings shall be taken against any member of the Force under this regulation in respect of any offence for which he has already been punished under any other provisions of these Regulations. (3) Nothing in the proviso to sub-regulation (2) shall be construed as limiting or restricting the powers conferred on the Commissioner-General by regulation 41 . (4) Any fire and rescue officer who is taken before a district court under sub-regulation (1) or any person suspected on reasonable grounds of being guilty of desertion may be arrested without a warrant by a fire and rescue officer of equal or senior rank.

provisions.title: Dismissal and reduction in rank of fire and rescue officer on conviction
provisions.content: The Commissioner-General or any disciplinary authority may reduce in rank or dismiss from the Force any fire and rescue officer who has been convicted by any court of any offence, whether under the Fire and Rescue Force Act or otherwise, unless such officer has successfully appealed against such conviction; and in the event of dismissal all arrears of pay due to such officer may be forfeited by order of the Commissioner General or any other appropriate disciplinary authority .

provisions.title: Pay not to accrue during imprisonment or absence
provisions.content: No pay shall accrue to any fire and rescue officer in respect of any period during which he is undergoing any sentence of imprisonment or during which he is absent without leave.

provisions.title: Interdiction and suspension
provisions.content: (1) An officer who is interdicted or suspended shall, with effect from the date of interdiction or suspension, cease to be entitled to receive any salary or other allowance— Provided that the officer may be paid during the period of his interdiction or suspension, an alimentary allowance in such amount and subject to such terms and conditions as the disciplinary authority may determine. (2) For the purpose of this regulation— (a) interdiction means the temporary removal of an officer from exercising the duties of his office while a disciplinary charge is being investigated; (b) suspension means the temporary removal of an officer from exercising the duties of his office after he has been convicted of a criminal offence under the Penal Code until such time as the result of the disciplinary proceedings against him are known.

provisions.title: Fine to be recovered by stoppage of pay
provisions.content: (1) All fines or payments awarded against a fire and rescue officer in respect of an offence under these Regulations may be recovered by stoppage of the offender's pay due at the time of committing such offence or thereafter accruing. (2) The amount of stoppage in respect of any fine or for any other cause authorized by these Regulations shall be at the discretion of the disciplinary authority but shall, in no case, exceed one half of the monthly pay of the offender; whenever more than one order of stoppage is in force against the same person, no stoppage exceeding one half of this pay shall be allowed. (3) Where more than one order of stoppage is made upon the same person, the orders later in date shall, if necessary, be postponed as to their enforcement until the earlier orders have been discharged. Part VI – Remuneration and allowances

provisions.title: Establishment of the Remuneration Board
provisions.content: (1) There is hereby established the Remuneration Board for Fire and Rescue Force . (2) The Board shall consist of— (a) the Minister who shall be a Chairman; (b) the Deputy Minister who shall be the Vice Chairman; (c) the Permanent Secretary who shall be the Secretary; (d) the Commissioner General; and (e) three members to be appointed by the Minister with the advice of the Commissioner-General . (3) The functions of the Board shall be to review the rates of remuneration packages periodically and advise the Government accordingly.

provisions.title: Allowances payable
provisions.content: (1) All fire and rescue officers shall be entitled to— (a) ration allowance; (b) hardship allowance; (c) transport allowance; (d) night duty allowance; and (e) any other allowances as may be applicable to the Force . (2) The following allowances shall be payable to eligible fire and rescue officers as may be prescribed— (a) professional allowance; (b) special duty allowance; (c) trade pay allowance; (d) outfit allowance; (e) mileage allowance; (f) instructors allowance; (g) disturbance allowance; (h) responsibility allowance; (i) acting allowance; (j) entertainment allowance; (k) subsistence allowance; (l) rent or housing maintenance allowance; and (m) batman allowance.

provisions.title: Other allowances for fire and rescue officers
provisions.content: Without prejudice to the foregoing provisions, fire and rescue officers shall be entitled to any other allowances as stipulated by circulars from the President's Office - Public Service Management. Part VII – Pensions and gratuities

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: The provisions of this Part shall apply to non-pensionable fire and rescue officers below the rank of Assistant Inspector.

provisions.title: When gratuity may be granted
provisions.content: A fire and rescue officer to whom this regulation applies shall, on discharge other than discharge on dismissal from the force for misconduct, after a continuous good service extending over a period of not less than three years, be eligible to receive a gratuity calculated at the rate of four-fifths of a month's pay of the rank of the officer in which he was serving at the date of his discharge for every completed year of service.

provisions.title: Where fire and rescue officer dies while on contract
provisions.content: Where a fire and rescue officer to whom any gratuity which would have been due and payable to him dies before he had taken his discharge under the provisions of these Regulations, such gratuity shall, in the event of his re-engaging for a further term and dying in the service before the completion of such term, be deemed to have belonged to him for the purposes of the administration of his estate.

provisions.title: Where death occurs before receipt of gratuity
provisions.content: Where a fire and rescue officer to whom this regulation applies having been discharged, dies before receipt by him of any gratuity for which he has become eligible under these Regulations, such gratuity shall be paid to the person authorized to administer his estate.

provisions.title: Recovery of debt from gratuity
provisions.content: Notwithstanding any of the provisions of these Regulations, where at the time of the discharge of a fire and rescue officer to whom this regulation applies from the Force , such officer has failed to pay any outstanding canteen and welfare bills owed by him to any canteen or welfare or any other institution of the Force , any gratuity payable under regulation 49 may, at any time, be withheld until satisfactory arrangements have been made for settlement of such bills as aforesaid either by him or by deduction from such gratuity.

provisions.title: Gratuity not assignable
provisions.content: Except as otherwise provided in these Regulations, a gratuity granted under regulation 48 shall not be assigned or transferred except for the purposes of satisfying— (a) a debt due to the Government; or (b) an order of any competent court for the periodical payment of sums or money towards the maintenance of the wife or former wife or child of the fire and rescue officer to whom the gratuity has been granted. Part VIII – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Certain fire and rescue officers not to resign without permission
provisions.content: No fire and rescue officer below the rank of Assistant Inspector shall be at liberty to resign while serving a period of engagement or reengagement unless expressly permitted to do so by the Commissioner General.

provisions.title: Resignation of fire and rescue officers of or above the rank of Assistant Inspector
provisions.content: No fire and rescue officer of, or above the rank of Assistant Inspector shall be at liberty to resign while in the service unless expressly permitted to do so by the appointing authority .

provisions.title: Prolongation of service in case of war, etc.
provisions.content: Notwithstanding any other provisions of these Regulations, any fire and rescue officer whose period of service expires during a state of war, insurrection or hostilities may be retained as the Commissioner General may direct.

provisions.title: Other interests prohibited
provisions.content: No fire and rescue officer shall, without the consent in writing of the Commissioner-General , engage in any employment or office whatsoever otherwise than in accordance with the duties imposed on fire and rescue officers by or under the Fire and Rescue Force Act .

provisions.title: Prohibition against joining trade unions, political parties and other associations
provisions.content: (1) No fire and rescue officer shall be or became a member of— (a) any political party, trade union or anybody or association affiliated to a trade union; or (b) anybody or association the object or one of the objects of which is to control or influence the pay, pensions or conditions of the service, other than a body or association which may be constituted and regulated pursuant to rules made by the Commissioner-General . (2) Any fire and rescue officer who contravenes the provisions of this regulation shall be guilty of misconduct and shall be liable to be dismissed from the force and forfeit all rights to any retirement benefits. (3) Where any questions arise as to whether anybody is a body or association to which this regulation applies, the question shall be decided by the Commissioner-General and his decision shall be final and conclusive.

provisions.title: Criminal offences in respect of disciplinary matters in grave or repeated offences
provisions.content: Where it appears to the officer investigating any charge that the offence would, by reason of the gravity or by reason of previous offences or for any other reason, not be adequately punished by any of the punishments mentioned in these Regulations, such officer shall defer awarding punishment and shall report the proceeding to the Commissioner-General and the Commissioner-General may return report for such further inquiry as he may deem necessary, or he may re-hear the case, or may order the accused to be taken before the district court and tried for such offence.

provisions.title: Where two or more disciplinary authorities have concurrent jurisdiction
provisions.content: (1) Where two or more disciplinary authorities have concurrent jurisdiction under these Regulations to institute disciplinary proceeding against an officer for the same offence and such proceedings have been commenced by any one of them, the officer shall not be subjected to disciplinary proceedings for the same offence by any other disciplinary authority : Provided that a disciplinary authority who is senior in rank may require any disciplinary authority who is junior to him in rank to transfer to him any disciplinary proceedings commenced by such disciplinary authority junior in rank, and upon such proceedings being transferred to him, the disciplinary authority senior in rank may proceed with it as if the proceedings had been instituted by him. (2) For the purpose of this regulation, the Commission shall be deemed to be the disciplinary authority most senior in rank. Part IX – Revocation and savings

provisions.title: Savings
provisions.content: (1) Notwithstanding the provisions of these Regulations, any other matters not covered in these Regulations shall be dealt with in accordance with Government Standing Orders, Staff Circulars and Fire and Rescue Force Standing Orders as may be issued. (2) Notwithstanding the provisions of any other regulations, where on the date coming into operation of these Regulations, any proceedings have been commenced under the former Regulations but have not been completed under the provisions of, and in accordance with the former Regulations, those proceedings shall continue under the former Regulations. definitions.definition: the Fire and Rescue Force Act

definitions.term: means— (a) an unmarried child, a child born out of wedlock, a step child, a legally adopted child or child of a deceased near relative who is not more than eighteen years of age and is wholly maintained by the fire and rescue officer ; (b) an unmarried child, a child born out of wedlock, a step child or a legally adopted child or child of deceased near relative of any age, who because of a physical or mental infirmity is unable to earn a living and is therefore wholly maintained by the fire and rescue officer ; (c) an unmarried child of any age who for reasons of undergoing studies at any recognized institution is unable to earn a living and is wholly maintained by the fire and rescue officer ; “ Commandant
definitions.definition: a fire and rescue officer appointed by the Commissioner-General to be the head of any training institution in the Force
a Commissioner-General appointed under section 14 of the Fire and Rescue Act

the Commissioners, Deputy Commissioners, Senior Assistant Commissioners, Regional Fire and Rescue Officers, District Fire and Rescue Officers and any other Fire and Rescue Officer to whom the powers have been delegated
an authority specified under section 7 of the Act

any act, omission, misconduct, failure to take reasonable care or failure to discharge duties in a reasonable satisfactory manner by or on the part of a fire and rescue officer , which in the opinion of the disciplinary authority , warrants disciplinary proceedings under these Regulations
to cause any person to become a member of the Fire and Rescue Force and includes re-engagement
a fire and rescue officer , his or her spouse and children of such an officer
a fireman appointed under the provisions of the Fire and Rescue Act
any fire and rescue officer who has attended and passed the gazetted officers course and has subsequently been promoted to the rank of Assistant Superintendent or above
an in-service fire and rescue officers' course leading to a promotion to a senior fire and rescue officer
the Commissioner-General or an officer appointed by the Commissioner-General to conduct an inquiry under these Regulations
an Inspector and includes an Assistant Inspector
the Minister responsible for matters relating to the fire and rescue services
a fire and rescue officer appointed by the Commissioner-General to be in-charge of a fire station
the Permanent Secretary of the Ministry responsible for matters relating to the fire and rescue services
any recognized university degree or other recognized equivalent qualification from a recognized university or institution
a Fire and Rescue Officer appointed by the Commissioner General to be the head of the fire and rescue services in the region
the attachment of a fire and rescue officer to any other public or private institution or post for specified period of time as may be determined by the appointing authority
a gazetted fire and rescue officer of or above the rank of Assistant Superintendent
the Fire and Rescue Force established under section 4 of the Fire and Rescue Force Act
a Fire and rescue officer of or below the rank of Inspector

any qualification or craft acquired by a fire and rescue officer from a recognized institution leading to the award of a certificate or diploma. Part II – Establishment and abolition of fire and rescue offices
